title: Tetrardisia bifaria
intro: Tetrardisia bifaria, also known as the 'Two-leaved Tetrardisia', is a shrub native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, belonging to the family Gelsemiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Tetrardisia bifaria include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Tetrardisia bifaria include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a variety of ailments in traditional medicine practices.
